meta.title: Pål Gøran Stolt-Larsen Pettersen — Sommelier & Wine Director
meta.description: Sommelier with experience from Noma, Sketch London, Tango Stavanger and Tempo Vinbar. Currently F&B Manager at Skjolden Hotel by the Sognefjord. Wine courses, talks and private wine events.

hero.subheading: It's in the hands that tend, the soil that gives, the moment that shares.
hero.title: F&B Manager, Skjolden Hotel · Sommelier & Wine Director

about.heading: From Sogn to London. And back.
about.p1: Pål Gøran Stolt-Larsen Pettersen has spent twenty years learning three things: where the wine comes from, how it tastes, and how to share it.
about.p2: From the cellars of Noma in Copenhagen, to the rooms of Sketch in London. From Tango Bar & Kjøkken in Stavanger, to his own Tempo Vinbar in Sandnes. Six kitchens. Five cities. One conviction — that wine, in the end, is about people.
about.p3: Now he's back where it all began — at Skjolden Hotel, by the Sognefjord. Not a retreat. A choice.
about.p4: It's never about the label. It's about the bottle — and the evening it's part of.
about.pull_quote: It's never about the label. It's about the evening.

timeline.heading: The cellars that shaped a career.

skjolden_now.description: Back to the fjord. From early career to full responsibility — twenty years later, with the world's wine scenes in his suitcase.

tempo.description: Co-founder of Tempo Vinbar in Sandnes. Built from scratch — one bottle, one guest, one evening at a time.

tango.description: Ten years at Stavanger's most celebrated fine dining. Quoted in VinePair on wine preferences and the local scene.

sketch.description: Sommelier at one of London's most iconic Michelin complexes. Where the international perspective was opened.

noma.description: Sommelier at the restaurant that redefined Nordic gastronomy. Where the understanding of produce, terroir and precision was shaped.

skjolden_start.description: Where it all began. Far from the noise. Close to the earth.

philosophy.heading: Terroir. Precision. Elegance.
philosophy.terroir_quote: Wine begins in the soil, not on the label.
philosophy.precision_quote: The right glass at the right moment requires knowledge — and instinct.
philosophy.access_quote: Good wine shouldn't be intimidating. It should be a door in.

regions.name: Burgundy
regions.subtitle: France · Côte d'Or
regions.tagline: Where Pinot Noir and Chardonnay reach their zenith.
regions.body: No region speaks the language of terroir louder than Burgundy. From the sharp limestone of Chablis to the grand cru vineyards of the Côte d'Or, every village carries its own signature. Two vineyards 200 metres apart can taste like two different worlds. This isn't about the grape. It's about the soil the grape grew in.

regions.name: Champagne
regions.subtitle: France · Reims & Épernay
regions.tagline: The only drink worthy of a true occasion.
regions.body: Champagne is precision in bubble form. Not the brands — but grower champagne. The houses where the vintage carries the voice of the land, not the marketing department. Salon. Jacques Selosse. Ulysse Collin. Forget brand thinking. Listen to the chalk, the limestone and the rain.

regions.name: South Africa
regions.subtitle: Swartland · Stellenbosch
regions.tagline: The old continent, the new voice.
regions.body: The old continent has found a new voice in the south. From the granite slopes of Swartland to the old Chenin Blanc vines of Stellenbosch, the country produces wines with an elegance matching the best of Europe — but with its own uncompromising identity. Mullineux. Sadie Family. Alheit. The names that show where the wine world is heading.

regions.name: Portugal
regions.subtitle: Douro · Dão · Vinho Verde
regions.body: Portuguese whites — especially from the Douro and Dão — are a revelation for those brave enough to leave the familiar behind. Encruzado, Arinto and Alvarinho make wines of structure, minerality and ageing potential few thought possible from the peninsula. Europe's next great white wine revolution has already begun. While the rest of the world talks about Burgundy, the Portuguese quietly smile in the cellar.

services.heading: Services and collaboration.
services.courses_desc: Tailored courses for companies and individuals. At home, at the hotel, or in the cellar.
services.talks_desc: Talks on wine, food and culture — with stories from the kitchens of Noma, Sketch and Tango.
services.events_desc: Private wine experiences — from four people around a table to weddings by the fjord.
services.consulting_desc: Wine lists, procurement and profile for restaurants that mean it.
services.sommelier_desc: Sommelier for one evening. Your home. Your occasion.

quote.text: In Stavanger, we are more classic. Champagne, red and white Burgundy, and German Riesling are all popular. Right now, the things I find most interesting are South African whites and reds, and Austrian Pinot Noir.
quote.author: Pål Gøran Stolt-Larsen Pettersen

skjolden.heading: Where the fjord ends and the road begins.
skjolden.ingress: Skjolden is not a place you end up. It's a place you choose.
skjolden.body: As F&B Manager at Skjolden Hotel, Pål is responsible for the entire food and beverage experience by the fjord. Wine list, procurement, hospitality, guests — everything he spent twenty years learning, gathered in one place.

tempo.heading: Four years. One bar. A thousand evenings.
tempo.body: From 2022 to 2026, Pål ran Tempo Vinbar in Sandnes. Built from scratch — one bottle, one guest, one evening at a time. Some chapters aren't endings. They're proof.

contact.heading: Get in touch
contact.subheading: For events, wine courses, consulting — or a conversation about wine.
contact.name: Name
contact.success: Thank you. Pål will be in touch soon.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again, or email directly.
